patterns.rule: Never create DateFormatter or ISO8601DateFormatter per call — use static let singleton
patterns.good_example: private static let isoFormatter: ISO8601DateFormatter = { let f = ISO8601DateFormatter(); f.formatOptions = [.withInternetDateTime, .withFractionalSeconds]; return f }()
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #12 #13 — Reviewer A: 'please stop creating Dateformatter and use a let instead'

patterns.rule: NEVER modify shared/base files used by other modules — revert any changes to CurrencyFormatter, shared models, etc.
patterns.bad_example: Modifying CurrencyFormatter.swift removingSpacesAroundSymbol() for Gold-specific AED formatting
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #3 — Reviewer A: 'Revert all this change please. They are used through the whole project.'

patterns.rule: Use enums/constants for known string values — never hardcode 'GOLD', 'AED', error codes
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #5 #8 #9 — Reviewer A: 'Don't you have an enum for GOLD?', 'Use a constant', 'use an enum for ONBOARDING_ALREADY_COMPLETE'

patterns.rule: Dangerous defaults — don't default currency/country to AED/AE. UK users would get wrong data. Make field required (failable init returns nil)
patterns.good_example: guard let currencyCode = network.currencyCode else { return nil }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #7 — Reviewer A: 'if the backend fail we will send wrong information to the user if we are in UK'

patterns.rule: Domain models should use typed enums, not raw strings — especially for status fields
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #6 — Reviewer A: 'Shouldn't it be a GoldOrderStatus?'

patterns.rule: Failable init should return nil for invalid data — empty string defaults hide backend failures
patterns.good_example: guard let accountName = network.accountName, !accountName.isEmpty else { return nil }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #4 — Reviewer A: 'does it make sense to have this item with no iban and no accountName?'

patterns.rule: ALL user-visible strings must use Localizable.strings — including number defaults, currency formatting, error messages
Use Constants for repeated header keys and magic strings
patterns.good_example: Headers.country: market.config.countryCode (where Headers is a constants enum)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #2 — Reviewer A: 'Please use a Constants for this key'

patterns.rule: Don't duplicate header logic — check if X-User-Id is already in a base request builder/interceptor
patterns.bad_example: Manually adding X-User-Id header in every request builder
patterns.good_example: Use base request builder that automatically includes common headers
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #1 — Reviewer A: 'Don't we already have this information somewhere in the header?'

patterns.rule: No duplicate assets — check DesignKit/Illustration/Gold/ before adding to Gold/ imageset
patterns.bad_example: gold_partner_goldwise in both DesignKit/Illustration/Gold/ AND Gold/
patterns.good_example: Single source: either DesignKit or Gold, not both
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #10 #11 — Reviewer A: 'You already have this asset', 'What is that?'

patterns.rule: Heavy images should be done programmatically if possible
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment Review #1 — Reviewer A: 'This one is very heavy. Can't we do it programmatically?'

patterns.rule: Static/singleton formatters — DateFormatter is one of the most expensive Foundation objects to instantiate
patterns.bad_example: private static func makeISOFormatter() -> ISO8601DateFormatter { return ISO8601DateFormatter() }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX + Reviewer A (Slack 2026-03-26): 'DateFormatter in Swift — is one of the most expensive objects to instantiate in Foundation. So please use Static / singleton per format'
